name: Gateway termination during streaming

injection.description: Send SIGTERM to one gateway instance (normal orchestrated pod termination) while it is relaying active streams.

expected_gateway_semantics: On SIGTERM (via the preStop drain hook of the deployment contract) readiness goes false and no new requests are accepted.
Accepted streams run to completion within the termination grace period — the deployment contract guarantees grace period > max stream duration, so no in-flight stream is force-killed.
The process exits only after in-flight streams have completed (or the grace deadline is reached, which the rule above makes unreachable for conformant streams).
expected_client_visible_behavior: In-flight streams complete normally; new requests are served by the remaining instances; zero client-visible errors attributable to the termination.

metrics_that_must_move.expectation: drains to 0 on the terminating instance before process exit
load-balancer/endpoint view of the terminating instance leaves rotation before its streams finish (drain ordering observable)

metrics_that_must_not_move.expectation: no status_class=5xx increase attributable to the termination window

abort_condition: Abort the injection (and restore the instance) if any in-flight stream is dropped without a standardized SSE error event, or if new requests are accepted after drain began.
